components.name: AppShell
components.purpose: Top-level four-pane layout (strip / sidebar / main / spine). Each of the sidebar and spine is independently togglable; when collapsed, a narrow pane-rail renders a single reveal IconButton. Keyboard: ⌘\ strip, ⌘[ sidebar, ⌘] spine. Carries a data-tab-style attribute (A / B / C) driven by the SurfaceDevPanel for live A/B of the tab register.

components.name: ProjectStrip
components.purpose: Vertical icon nav across projects. Top: project squares (with a pulsing activity dot when any workspace in that project is active or needs attention). Middle: transparent IconButton for New project. Bottom cluster: Settings + Help (each opens the AppDialog). No quick-switcher icon — ⌘K is the only entry point.

components.name: WorkspaceSidebar
components.purpose: Sits directly on the page surface (no fill, no border) per the two-tier register. Project title (self-describing name + path) + collapse IconButton + Home button + Workspaces list (status icon + name, single line — branch name lives in the row's title attribute for hover). Each row reveals an Archive IconButton on hover/focus-within (workspace-row__actions slot). Below the active list, a collapsible Archived (N) group pins to the bottom of the sidebar via flex-column auto-margin; archived rows surface Restore + Delete IconButtons. Via WorkspaceStatusIcon, the 7-variant status set reads the same here as on HomeTabA. Pane resizer sits at the floating-surface edge (cursor-only feedback; no visible bar). 13.E: when the active workspace has at least one mergeable track, a Request Merge IconButton (GitPullRequest) renders inline next to the project title — runs cmd_request_merge on the most recent eligible track.

components.name: MainView
components.purpose: Routes between project-home and workspace view. Wraps the tab body (or home surface) in a .main-surface rectangle — the only Tier-2 raised surface on the page. Tabs bar sits above the surface on the page; the active tab merges into the surface top edge (styles A + B) or floats separately (style C) based on the [data-tab-style] attribute carried by .app-shell. Project-home topbar occupies the same vertical slot as the tabs-bar and carries only the Panels/Palette variant toggle.

components.name: TabLayout
components.purpose: Tab body primitive: scrollable content region + optional bottom dock slot for compose-style inputs. Matched max-width between scroll and dock so the dock feels aligned with content, not anchored to window edges.

components.name: ActivitySpine
components.purpose: Sits directly on the page surface (no fill, no border) per the two-tier register. Right-rail hierarchical status column, scoped to the active project (narrows further to the active workspace when one is selected). Events are filtered to streams that belong to the scope. Summary renders as dot+count per non-zero state; zero-state fallback is 'Nothing streaming'. Rows are text-only with per-depth repeating-linear-gradient indent rails. Header carries a collapse IconButton (⌘]) and a hover-revealed .pane-toggle on the left edge.

components.name: QuickSwitcher
components.purpose: Cmd+K fuzzy search across projects and workspaces. Dialog with focus trap.

components.name: HomeTabA
components.purpose: Project home — single-column dashboard. Sections in order: Needs your attention (only when non-empty), Near-term focus, Active workspaces (WorkspaceStatusIcon + name + one-line summary from the first open tab), Recent reports, Autonomy (interactive SegmentedToggle with optimistic local update via setAutonomyOverride — Phase 13 will swap in IPC). No kicker. Panels, not cards; hairline dividers between groups. The Home A/B toggle was pruned 2026-04-22 — Panels is committed.

components.name: HomeTabB
components.purpose: Retired 2026-04-23. The palette variant of project home was consolidated into Variant A (Panels). File deleted; entry retained for history.

components.name: WorkspaceThread
components.purpose: The single tab body for every workspace tab post-Phase 13.1 (spec Decision 36). One continuous scrollable thread of typed artifact blocks (resolved via the block registry) plus a docked ComposeDock. Per-tab `hasStarted` state — fresh tabs render context-aware suggestions sourced from the workspace's recent artifacts; first send flips to thread mode. Subscribes to `artifact_*` stream events filtered to the production wire format `workspace:<uuid>` (also tolerates the bare-uuid mock format) for live refresh. Phase 13.D wired `onSend` to `ipcClient().postMessage` with synchronous `useRef` re-entry guard against double-dispatch, draft restoration via the ComposeDock imperative handle on failure, and typed `IpcError` translation via `ipc/error.ts` (cost-cap-exceeded / scope-denied / etc. each get their own copy).

components.name: PlanTab
components.purpose: Retired 2026-04-24 (spec Decision 36). Plan/Design/Build/Blank tab types collapsed into a single WorkspaceThread surface. File deleted.

components.name: DesignTab
components.purpose: Retired 2026-04-24 (spec Decision 36). File deleted.

components.name: BuildTab
components.purpose: Retired 2026-04-24 (spec Decision 36). Chat + slash-commands behavior absorbed into WorkspaceThread + ComposeDock. File deleted.

components.name: BlankTab
components.purpose: Retired 2026-04-24 (spec Decision 36). Empty-state behavior is now the suggestions view inside WorkspaceThread. File deleted.

components.name: BlockRenderers
components.purpose: DP-B (2026-04-30) pass-through pivot: chat is Claude-Code-by-default. Five custom renderers — MessageBlock (asymmetric authorship: user bubble vs. agent flat), ApprovalBlock (the must-intercept inline card), CommentBlock (left-rule attached comment), ToolUseLine (terse one-line register for tool-use reports — replaces the old ToolCallGroup disclosure; Phase 23.C 2026-05-02 expand-to-payload renders the full artifact body as monospace `<pre>` inside an aria-live region wrapper that also carries loading / loaded / error phases so payload arrival is layout-stable; >40 lines truncates with a `Show full` disclosure; getArtifact rejection surfaces `Nothing to show.` + a `Try again` retry button instead of an empty box; per-mount cache + in-flight ref dedupes fast double-clicks; mountedRef guard so the IPC promise can't write state after unmount; head renders a lucide-react `ChevronRight` (size 12, strokeWidth 1.5) that rotates 90° on `aria-expanded=true` so the row's interactivity is visible at rest, not just on hover), ArtifactReferenceBlock (one-line `→ Spec: …` clickable reference; click dispatches `designer:focus-artifact` so ActivitySpine scrolls + flashes the matching row). ReportBlock dispatches: tool-use titles → ToolUseLine, recap/auditor → ArtifactReferenceBlock. Rich artifact kinds (spec, code-change, pr, task-list, prototype) all register to ArtifactReferenceBlock. Stub renderers diagram/variant/track-rollup unregistered (DP-C audit, no payload source); GenericBlock fallback (also ArtifactReferenceBlock) catches stray kinds. ApprovalBlock parses inline JSON for `approval_id`, calls `cmd_resolve_approval` on Grant/Deny with optimistic flip + projector truth via approval_granted/denied stream subscription.

components.name: ComposeDock
components.purpose: Shared compose surface for WorkspaceThread (and any future tab variants). Owns its own draft, attachments, model/effort/plan-mode controls, and drag-and-drop target. Imperative handle (`setDraft`, `focus`) lets the parent seed prompts and restore drafts on send failure (used by WorkspaceThread.onSend's catch handler — the dock clears its draft synchronously after `onSend` returns, so failure recovery has to re-seed via the handle). 13.D wired `onSend` through to `Orchestrator::post_message` via `cmd_post_message`; the parent is responsible for in-flight gating and error display. tab-ui-bugs (2026-05-02) added `initialDraft` (seeds the draft on mount from a parent-managed cache) and `onDraftChange` (fires on every keystroke, paste, programmatic setDraft, and post-send clear) so callers can persist per-tab drafts in the app store and survive tab switches. Phase 23.B (2026-05-03) added optional `workspaceId` + `tabId` props that pin a `ComposeDockActivityRow` above the textarea — the row reads the per-tab activity slice and renders pulse + elapsed counter (Working) or 'Approve to continue' (AwaitingApproval).

components.name: ComposeDockActivityRow
components.purpose: Phase 23.B — pinned status row above the compose textarea. Subscribes to the per-tab activity slice (keyed by `${workspace_id}:${tab_id}`) and renders three states: `idle` hides the row entirely; `working` shows a pulsing accent dot + 'Working… {MM:SS|H:MM:SS}' counter (mono, tabular-nums); `awaiting_approval` shows a warning-tinted pulse + 'Approve to continue' with a chevron pointing at the inbox. Translates Rust enum names to user-facing copy on the frontend; the Rust DTO never carries the user-facing strings. The 1Hz elapsed counter only ticks while state == 'working' — no setInterval cost during AwaitingApproval. Reduced motion: the pulse keyframe is explicitly overridden to `animation: none` under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` (chosen over relying on axioms.css's collapse-to-0.01ms so the T-23B-3 acceptance test reads naturally with `getComputedStyle`). Phase 23.F (2026-05-03) added a Stop button (StopCircle, aria-label='Stop turn') that renders only while state == 'working'; click dispatches `ipcClient.interruptTurn(workspaceId, tabId)` and optimistically hides the row — the authoritative `ActivityChanged{Idle}` from the orchestrator clears the slice. Tab-order: composer textarea → Stop. Documented in pattern-log.md.

components.name: SettingsPage
components.purpose: Full-page Settings surface (replaces the modal that previously lived in AppDialog). Settings is global-only per Decision 63: left rail of section anchors (Appearance, Account, Models, Preferences, Activity) hosts only installation-wide concerns (theme, Claude Code account + Keychain, cost-chip preference, feature flags, friction triage, Designer noticed). Per-project concerns (linked repo, autonomy override) live in Project Home. Mounted from App.tsx when `dialog === 'settings'`; takes over the whole shell viewport. Esc closes; AppDialog now renders Help only.

components.name: SurfaceDevPanel
components.purpose: Dev-only floating panel (⌘.) that exposes seven knobs over the surface register: Compose fill, Main tab fill, Surface sand, Tab opacity, Border intensity, Shadow intensity, plus a Tab-corners variant toggle (Soft/Concentric/Folder/Match/Custom) backed by a 0-32px slider, and Main tab + Compose radius sliders. Persists to localStorage. Only mounted when `import.meta.env.MODE === 'development'`; the production build never ships it.

components.name: StreamingText
components.purpose: Claude-style character-by-character text reveal with blink cursor. Reduced-motion falls back to instant.

components.name: Tooltip
components.purpose: Custom-popover tooltip that replaces the HTML title attribute. Appears immediately on hover and on keyboard focus, renders in a fixed-position layer anchored via getBoundingClientRect (no overflow clipping), supports a separate shortcut slot rendered as right-aligned kbd, announces via aria-describedby. 'auto' placement flips to top or bottom based on available room. Reduced-motion skips the entrance animation.

components.name: IconButton
components.purpose: Single icon-only button archetype. Two sizes (sm 24×24 for dense inline affordances, md 32×32 for navigation/topbar/compose). Required tooltip label (wraps children in Tooltip), optional shortcut, optional pressed state, transparent by default with a btn-icon--primary modifier for the filled Send variant. Focus ring on the outer button so the entire hit target receives focus.

components.name: SegmentedToggle
components.purpose: Two-to-N pill toggle. Collapses the identical Panels/Palette variant toggle (MainView) and Bounded/Open density toggle (Palette) into one component. Each option takes a value, label, optional tooltip, and optional icon. CSS lives under `.segmented-toggle` / `.segmented-toggle__btn`.

components.name: Icons
components.purpose: Thin wrappers around lucide-react for the canonical set reused 3+ times across the app: IconX, IconPlus, IconBranch, IconChevronLeft/Right, IconCollapseLeft/Right. Defaults size and strokeWidth per axiom #13 (12/14/16 px, 1.25 stroke at sm/md, 1.5 at lg). One-off icons are imported from lucide-react directly in the consumer file, passing size/strokeWidth inline.

components.name: WorkspaceStatusIcon
components.purpose: Single source of truth for the 7-variant workspace status glyph. Used by WorkspaceSidebar rows and HomeTabA's Active-workspaces section so status reads identically everywhere. Each status maps to a Lucide icon and a semantic-color CSS variable on .workspace-status.

components.name: Palette
components.purpose: Shared palette primitive — prompt + 4–6 context-aware suggestions. Default consumer is BlankTab (workspace-scoped). Default density is 'open' — bare input on the surface with a blinking caret, no container; suggestions render directly on the surface. 'bounded' density stays as a runtime alternative for surfaces that want prompt + suggestions in a single rounded object.

components.name: PaneResizer
components.purpose: Edge drag handle for the workspace sidebar and activity spine. Pointer-captured drag writes in-memory via `onLiveChange` (no localStorage during drag); pointer-up calls `onCommit` to flush the latest width to disk. Double-click resets to default and commits. Arrow keys ±16px (±48px with shift) commit immediately. Width clamps to [180, 480]. `role="separator"` + `aria-valuemin/max/now`.

components.name: AppDialog
components.purpose: Modal for Help. Settings moved to the full-page SettingsPage (Decision 63); AppDialog now renders only when `dialog === 'help'`. Help body: keyboard-shortcut dl (⌘K / ⌘T / ⌘W / ↵ / ⇧↵ / ⌘\ / ⌘[ / ⌘] / ⌘⇧F / ⌘?), a Report-issues section with an `.app-dialog__inline-link` button to launch Friction (closes Help and toggles the composer), and an About row. The previous 'Ask the help agent' input was removed per FB-0036 (no half-baked features in prod). Inner row dividers use --color-border-soft so they don't double against the outer dialog frame. Scrim click and Escape close. Triggered from the ProjectStrip bottom cluster (Help IconButton) and the ⌘? shortcut.

components.name: Onboarding
components.purpose: Three-slide first-run introduction. The final slide's primary action launches RepoLinkModal when a workspace already exists; otherwise it falls back to a plain 'Get started'. Dismissible; persists in localStorage.

components.name: RepoLinkModal
components.purpose: Phase 13.E. Action-attached form modal that collects an absolute path to a git work-tree, validates it via cmd_link_repo (canonicalizes + validate_repo), and persists the link on the active workspace. Surfaces from the Onboarding final slide and from Project Home → Repository (per Decision 63 it no longer surfaces from Settings → Account). Reuses the existing app-dialog__* token-driven CSS — no new tokens. A11y: role=dialog + aria-modal, aria-labelledby, focus on input on open, Escape dismiss, Tab/Shift-Tab focus trap, scrim dismiss via onClick (not onMouseDown so a drag inside the dialog ending on the scrim doesn't surprise-dismiss). Errors render through role=alert and aria-describedby on the input.

components.name: RepoUnlinkModal
components.purpose: Confirmation modal for severing Designer's pointer to a project's linked repo. Surfaces from Project Home → Repository (Decision 63). Accepts an array of `workspaceIds` and fans out `cmd_unlink_repo` per workspace on confirm — the project-scoped UX collapses N workspace-level severances into one project-level action while the underlying data model stays per-workspace. Repo files on disk are untouched. Reuses the existing app-dialog__* token-driven CSS — no new tokens. A11y: role=dialog + aria-modal, aria-labelledby, aria-describedby on the body copy, initial focus on **Cancel** (destructive-confirm convention — keyboard Enter on first open lands on the safe path), Escape dismiss, Tab/Shift-Tab focus trap, scrim dismiss via onClick.

components.name: ComponentCatalog
components.purpose: Live tile grid showing each Mini-inspired component and its token binding, for the Design tab.

components.name: PrototypePreview
components.purpose: Sandboxed iframe rendering agent-produced prototype HTML. Two prop forms: { workspace } drives the lab demo (variant explorer + annotation layer); { inlineHtml, title? } is the artifact-thread form used by PrototypeBlock — bare iframe with sandbox="" and CSP <meta> injected for defense-in-depth.

components.name: AnnotationLayer
components.purpose: Pin-drop overlay on top of the prototype iframe. Click empty canvas to drop a pin; type a note; save. Every saved pin is a numbered button that opens a popover with the note, author, and a Remove action. Draft popover supports Enter-to-save, Escape-to-cancel, explicit Cancel button.

components.name: VariantExplorer
components.purpose: Variant selector for the prototype preview.

components.name: CostChip
components.purpose: Phase 13.G workspace topbar widget. Shows `<spent> / <cap>` with a colored band (50% green / 80% amber / >80% red, dimmed when no cap). Click expands a popover with daily / weekly / per-track breakdown (week + per-track placeholders defer to a follow-up). Hidden by default per spec Decision 34; the COST_CHIP_PREFERENCE_EVENT custom event triggers a re-fetch when Settings flips the toggle. Subscribes to the `cost_recorded` stream event so the chip updates per turn without explicit refresh.

components.name: KeychainStatusReadout
components.purpose: Phase 13.G Settings → Account row. Polls `cmd_get_keychain_status` once on mount and renders a stable copy + state dot (connected / disconnected / unsupported_os). `aria-live='polite'` so screen readers don't get re-announced on minor state churn. Decision 26 — the readout never reads or writes the secret bytes, only confirms the credential's presence in the macOS Keychain.

components.name: CostChipToggle
components.purpose: Phase 13.G Settings → Preferences row. Reuses SegmentedToggle for an Off / On chooser bound to `cmd_set_cost_chip_preference` (persists `cost_chip_enabled` in `~/.designer/settings.json`). Dispatches the `COST_CHIP_PREFERENCE_EVENT` custom event after writing so the topbar `CostChip` re-renders without a full reload.

components.name: PreTabSessionBanner
components.purpose: Phase 23.E migration notice. Top-center floating pill that fires once per install for users upgrading from a pre-23.E build (where claude sessions were workspace-scoped and SESSION_NAMESPACE was rotated to invalidate them). Detection signal: any project carries at least one workspace — the cheap, reliable proxy for 'had Designer before the upgrade.' Fresh installs land on the empty Home and never see the banner. Dismissal persists in localStorage (mirroring Onboarding) so a relaunch doesn't replay it. Escape and the 'Got it' button both dismiss. Sits below Onboarding's modal layer so the two never overlap; aria-live polite (not a dialog) so it never traps focus. One-and-done by design — re-evaluate if a third banner-like surface lands and a shared archetype becomes warranted.

components.name: UpdatePrompt
components.purpose: DP-A auto-updater surface. Bottom-left floating pill that probes the GitHub Releases manifest via tauri-plugin-updater on launch and surfaces a non-blocking notice when a newer version is available. Three states: available (Update now / Later), applying (with a 60s deadline that flips to error so a hung download doesn't freeze the UI), error (dismiss). Update path downloads + installs the signed bundle, then triggers tauri-plugin-process relaunch. Renders only inside the Tauri runtime; dynamic-imports the plugins so vitest builds stay clean. Dismissable for the session; a fresh launch re-probes.

components.name: FrictionButton
components.purpose: Track 13.M demoted bottom-right capture trigger. Visually quiet (smaller target-sm, no accent fill, opacity-led hover) because ⌘⇧F is the primary trigger; the button exists as the discoverable affordance for users who don't yet know the shortcut. Owns the global ⌘⇧F keybind so it survives Widget/Overlay unmount. Click toggles the composer open/closed; data-active reflects mode !== "off". Hidden while a modal scrim is open. Bottom-right corner remains permanently reserved per pattern-log.

components.name: SelectionOverlay
components.purpose: Track 13.M opt-in anchor mode (entered via ⌘. or 📍 button inside the composer). Renders nothing when frictionMode !== "selecting" or while a dialog is open. Listens to pointermove / click / keydown at the document level; uses requestAnimationFrame + ref-equality guard so re-renders fire only when the snap target crosses an element boundary. Walks ancestors to nearest data-component / role="row"|"button" / <button> / <dialog>; Alt overrides snap to the atomic node. Three-exit policy: ESC (back to composer, draft preserved), click an anchorable element (capture + return to composer with chip), click-outside after a 50ms post-arming suppression window (the silent 600ms grace from 13.K is gone). Banner carries a persistent legend so Alt-overrides-snap is discoverable.

components.name: FrictionWidget
components.purpose: Track 13.M composer-by-default surface. Mounts bottom-right above the demoted FrictionButton; body textarea autofocused on open (⌘⇧F). Body alone is enough to submit — when the user submits without anchoring, a page-level dom-element anchor synthesized from the active route stands in. Persistent key-hint footer (⌘↵ submit · ⌘⇧S screenshot · ⌘. anchor · ESC dismiss). Header carries 📍 anchor + camera capture + close icons. Optional anchor chip (× to clear) renders above the textarea when set. Screenshot inputs: ⌘⇧S (Tauri shells to screencapture; composer hides for one paint frame so it doesn't appear in its own screenshot), paste, drop, file picker. Stream-subscribed toast: "Filed locally" → "Filed as #abc123" once friction_reported lands in the projection (subscription managed by useEffect tied to submittedId). Hidden while a modal scrim is open. The widget stays mounted across mode flips (returns null) so component state survives entering/exiting selection mode without store round-tripping.

components.name: Titlebar
components.purpose: Top-of-window drag region for the Tauri shell. Mounted at App-level (not inside AppShell) so SettingsPage and modals inherit the same drag surface without each rendering their own. Belt-and-suspenders wiring — both `data-tauri-drag-region` and an explicit `startDragging()` mousedown handler stay live because the runtime's auto-detection silently failed during PR #24 dogfood.

components.name: CreateProjectModal
components.purpose: Path-first project-creation dialog (replaces the broken `window.prompt` flow — Tauri's bundled webview returns null). Path field fronts a native folder picker via `pickFolder()` per FB-0032; free-text fallback exists for power users but is never the only way in. Inline validation via `cmd_validate_project_path` on each edit; submit calls `cmd_create_project` (expands `~`, validates the path is a real directory, canonicalizes). Optional `onCreated` callback lets onboarding chain into a follow-up step; default behavior is `selectProject(id)`. Standard modal a11y — role=dialog, aria-modal, focus trap, scrim onClick (not onMouseDown per FB-0029), Escape dismiss.

components.name: DesignerNoticedPage
components.purpose: Phase 21.A1.2 proposal archive at Settings → Activity → "Designer noticed" (sibling to Friction). Renders proposals — recommendations the synthesis pass produces at boundaries — across all statuses with severity / kind / status filters. Findings appear only as collapsed evidence under each proposal's "from N observations" disclosure (never as their own list). Composes `ProposalRow` + `useProposals`. Severity drives the left-border accent only — info / notice / warn map to border tokens, not background fills, per the calm-by-default axiom.

components.name: DesignerNoticedHome
components.purpose: Phase 21.A1.2 workspace-home surface for proposals. Renders the top-N severity-sorted *open* proposals (Warn > Notice > Info, then most-recent) for the active project at the bottom of HomeTabA; the Settings → Activity → Designer noticed page is the full archive across statuses. Boundary-cadence: refreshes only on `ProposalEmitted` events (track-complete + first-view-of-day), never on `FindingRecorded`. Capped at MAX_HOME_PROPOSALS (8); composes `ProposalRow` + `useProposals` so the row shape stays consistent with the archive.

components.name: FindingRow
components.purpose: Retired in Phase 21.A1.2 (surface rework: proposals over findings). The row shape that surfaced individual findings as user-facing units was replaced by `ProposalRow`, which renders proposals (recommendations) and inlines source-finding evidence behind a "from N observations" disclosure. No live consumers remain; this entry is a tombstone so the manifest's path-coverage check still recognizes the historical name.

components.name: ProposalRow
components.purpose: Phase 21.A1.2 shared row used by both `DesignerNoticedPage` (archive) and `DesignerNoticedHome` (workspace-home open list). Renders a proposal — severity dot, title, summary, "from N observations" disclosure (expandable evidence drawer with source `Finding.summary` lines), Accept / Edit / Dismiss / Snooze actions, optional `calibrated 👍/👎` badge after thumbing. The user thumbs the *proposal*, never the finding; findings appear inline only inside the evidence drawer. Severity carries as a `data-severity` attribute on the row element; visual treatment lives entirely in CSS. Exports `sortProposalsForHome` for the home surface's severity-then-recency ordering.
